validation_results.name: schema runtime and downstream JP1 validation

consumer_evidence: Server provider import/export schemas were consumed by later service/API tasks and passed JP1 plus final server gates.

must_preserve_evidence.G_SEC_REDIRECT: ProviderBundleCredentialSchema separates redacted/missing/present credential states; ProviderBundleSchema rejects credential status 'present' when containsSecrets is false.
must_preserve_evidence.G_NO_ACTIVE: New schemas do not define activeId, default provider selection, active provider env, OAuth, or login state. Strict bundle/request schemas reject unknown active/default fields.
must_preserve_evidence.G_SCOPE: ProviderBundleProviderSchema models saved provider record fields only and includes sourceProviderId only as optional diagnostic/source metadata.
must_preserve_evidence.MP_001_to_MP_010: Schemas cover provider-only bundles, secret-free export, explicit secret export confirmation, preview diagnostics, commit resolutions, conflicts, and no active/default transfer.
must_preserve_evidence.CA_001_CA_003_CA_006: Default export schema supports server-owned redaction; import/commit schemas omit activation state; diagnostics and summaries avoid raw credential exposure except the explicit credential-bearing bundle path.

concerns: Full bun run check:server did not complete within the task timeout; focused same-area tests and schema checks passed.

recovery_hints: When later behavior tasks wire routes/services, use ProviderExportRequestSchema for secret-free export and ProviderSecretExportConfirmationRequestSchema for the dangerous credential-bearing route.
Commit implementation should preserve this schema decision: no activeId/default selection in import/export payloads or commit results.
If maintainers require full TypeScript verification, repair/install bun-types or pin the TypeScript invocation to the repository-supported version first.
